prompt: Convert the video E:\Private\skill开发\ffmpeg-video-workspace\test-files\test_input.mp4 to WebM format, saving it as E:\Private\skill开发\ffmpeg-video-workspace\iteration-1\eval-convert-format\with_skill\outputs\output.webm. Use the FFmpeg Python wrapper from E:\Private\skill开发\ffmpeg-skill\ (the package is named 'ffmpeg-skill' and you need to import it via importlib: import importlib, sys; sys.path.insert(0, r'E:\Private\skill开发'); mod = importlib.import_module('ffmpeg-skill'); FFmpegSkill = mod.FFmpegSkill). Use VP9 video codec and Opus audio codec.

assertions.name: output-webm-exists
assertions.description: Output WebM file exists and has size > 0 bytes

assertions.name: output-webm-valid
assertions.description: Output is a valid WebM file (ffprobe can read it)

assertions.name: output-has-vp9-video
assertions.description: Video stream is encoded with VP9 codec (libvpx-vp9)

assertions.name: output-has-opus-audio
assertions.description: Audio stream is encoded with Opus codec (libopus)
